title: Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek — What Are These AI Models and Which Is Best for You?
summary: Forjit AI uses open-source AI models — Llama 3.3, Qwen 3, and DeepSeek V3. Here's what each one is good at and when to use which.
content:
# Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek — What Are These AI Models?

Forjit AI gives you a choice of AI models — Llama 3.3 70B, Qwen 3 32B, and DeepSeek V3. If you're unsure which to use, this guide explains the differences simply.

## What Is an AI Model?

An AI model (also called a Large Language Model or LLM) is a system trained on enormous amounts of text to understand and generate human language. Think of it as a very capable auto-complete that can write, reason, and code.

Unlike ChatGPT (which uses OpenAI's proprietary model), Forjit AI uses **open-source** models — meaning their code and weights are publicly available, and anyone can run them.

## Llama 3.3 70B (by Meta)

**Made by:** Meta (Facebook's parent company)
**Parameters:** 70 billion
**Best for:** General tasks — writing, planning, documents, meal plans

Llama 3.3 is Meta's flagship open-source model. It handles complex instructions well, writes naturally, and is reliable for generating resumes, cover letters, and content planners.

**On Forjit AI:** Default model. Best choice if you're unsure.

## Qwen 3 32B (by Alibaba)

**Made by:** Alibaba (China's tech giant)
**Parameters:** 32 billion
**Best for:** Coding, structured output, web apps

Qwen 3 is excellent at generating clean, functional code. If you're using Forjit AI to build web apps or tools with complex logic, Qwen 3 often produces tighter, more functional code than larger models.

**On Forjit AI:** Best for the "Create App" tab.

## DeepSeek V3 (by DeepSeek)

**Made by:** DeepSeek AI (Chinese AI research lab)
**Parameters:** ~685 billion (mixture of experts)
**Best for:** Reasoning, complex analysis, research

DeepSeek caused a global stir in early 2025 when it matched GPT-4 performance at a fraction of the training cost. Its V3 model is excellent at multi-step reasoning and detailed analysis.

**On Forjit AI:** Best for complex documents, detailed reports, technical content.

## Quick Comparison

| Model | Speed | Writing | Coding | Reasoning |
|-------|-------|---------|--------|-----------|
| Llama 3.3 70B | Fast | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Qwen 3 32B | Very fast | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| DeepSeek V3 | Fast | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |

## Are These Models Safe?

All three are open-source — their behaviour has been studied by researchers worldwide. They're accessed via Groq and OpenRouter, which have their own safety filters.

**Important:** Your API key goes directly from your browser to Groq/OpenRouter. Forjit AI never sees your key or your prompts. See our [Privacy Policy](/privacy) for full details.

## Which Should You Choose?

- **Just trying things out:** Llama 3.3 70B (default)
- **Building a web app:** Qwen 3 32B
- **Complex document/analysis:** DeepSeek V3
- **Fastest response:** Llama 3.1 8B (for simple tasks)
